verdict.summary: Workflow failed 7 gating check(s). Resolve every reason below and re-run.

reasons.title: 10 HIGH-severity finding(s) present
reasons.detail: HIGH findings are release blockers regardless of category.
remediation: Open the Findings section below and resolve every HIGH item.
Re-run this auditor; the verdict should flip to PASS once all HIGH items are cleared.

reasons.title: 8 MEDIUM-severity findings (threshold: 5)
reasons.detail: A cluster of MEDIUM findings indicates systemic gaps (no retries, no error handling, weak hygiene).
remediation: Triage MEDIUM findings by category; treat each category as a single fix campaign.
Apply 'Retry on Fail' + 'Continue on Error' on all risky nodes (HTTP, DB, LLM).
Strip unused / disabled nodes; rename unclear ones.

reasons.title: No Error Workflow configured
reasons.detail: Without an Error Workflow, runtime failures are silent -- no alerts, no audit trail.
remediation: Create a dedicated Error Trigger workflow that logs the error payload and sends a notification (Slack/email/PagerDuty).
In Workflow Settings -> Error Workflow, select the workflow you just created.
Re-export and re-run the audit to confirm.

reasons.title: 1 webhook/trigger node(s) without authentication
reasons.detail: Public unauthenticated webhooks can be abused for spam, DoS, data poisoning, or as a pivot into downstream systems.
remediation: On each flagged Webhook node, set 'Authentication' to Header Auth / Basic Auth / JWT, or implement HMAC signature verification.
Rotate the webhook URL path to an unguessable value if it was ever public.
Add rate-limiting / IP allow-listing at the reverse proxy if exposed to the internet.

reasons.title: Untrusted input may reach 1 AI node(s): Node1
reasons.detail: A graph path exists from a trigger node to an LLM node. Anything a caller sends can influence the prompt (OWASP LLM01:2025).
remediation: Sanitize and strictly validate all user-controlled fields before they reach the LLM (allowlist, length cap, type checks).
Use a system prompt that explicitly refuses to follow instructions found inside user data.
Add an output guardrail step (regex / classifier / second LLM judge) before any side-effecting action.
Where possible, place untrusted text inside a clearly delimited block and instruct the model to treat it as data only.

reasons.title: 7 hardcoded credential/secret finding(s)
reasons.detail: Secrets stored directly in workflow JSON travel with every export, every backup, and every git commit.
remediation: ROTATE the exposed credential immediately at the provider (assume it is leaked).
Recreate the credential inside n8n's credential store.
Replace the literal in the node with an expression that references the credential.
Add a pre-commit hook / CI step that runs this auditor or gitleaks to prevent recurrence.

reasons.title: pinData (test fixtures) is shipped in the workflow export
reasons.detail: Importers inherit pinned test data, which masks real upstream values during testing and may leak data captured during development.
remediation: In the n8n UI, right-click each pinned node and 'Unpin Data' before exporting.
Alternatively, delete the top-level `pinData` key from the JSON before publishing.

findings.title: Webhook/trigger node 'Webhook' has no authentication
findings.recommendation: Enable header/basic/JWT auth or signature verification (e.g. HMAC).

findings.title: Hardcoded AWS Access Key ID
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded Stripe live key
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub PAT
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded Bearer token
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: Hardcoded OpenAI API key
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: Untrusted input may reach AI node 'Node1'
findings.detail: A trigger node has a directed path to this LLM node; inputs must be treated as untrusted.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize / validate inputs, add a system-prompt guardrail, and apply an output filter.

findings.title: Sensitive key 'password' holds a literal value
findings.recommendation: Move this value to an n8n credential or env var; reference it via expression instead of hardcoding.

findings.title: Hardcoded JWT
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: No Error Workflow configured
findings.recommendation: Create an Error Trigger workflow that logs + notifies on failures, and attach it here.

findings.title: Webhook 'Webhook' uses an overly broad path
findings.recommendation: Use a specific, unguessable path; avoid wildcards on public webhooks.

findings.title: Risky node 'HTTP Request' has neither retryOnFail nor continueOnFail
findings.recommendation: Enable Retry on Fail, set a timeout, and consider Continue On Error.

findings.title: Risky node 'Node1' has neither retryOnFail nor continueOnFail
findings.recommendation: Configure retries, fallback model, and polling for long tasks.

findings.title: AI node 'Node1' prompt has no obvious guardrail language
findings.detail: None of the configured guardrail keywords detected in the prompt.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit safety / refusal instructions and an output-validation step.

findings.title: Risky node 'Postgres' has neither retryOnFail nor continueOnFail
findings.recommendation: Wrap DB ops with Continue On Error and validate inputs.

findings.title: Risky node 'Dangling Cleanup' has neither retryOnFail nor continueOnFail
findings.recommendation: Custom Code nodes throw on runtime errors; wrap in try/catch and use Continue On Error.

findings.title: Node 'Dangling Cleanup' is unreachable (no incoming connections)
findings.recommendation: Delete the node or connect it from upstream.

findings.title: Workflow exports pinned test data
findings.detail: `pinData` is present -- importers will inherit stale fixtures.
findings.recommendation: Strip pinData before publishing the workflow JSON.

findings.title: Company identifier 'Axos' found in workflow
findings.recommendation: Confirm this identifier is safe to ship; consider parameterizing.

findings.title: Hardcoded email address
findings.recommendation: Rotate the credential immediately if real, and store via n8n credentials.

findings.title: AI node 'Node1' has no retryOnFail
findings.recommendation: Enable retryOnFail with a small delay; configure a fallback model.

findings.title: Duplicate node name 'Node1' (2 occurrences)
findings.recommendation: Give each node a unique name for unambiguous debugging.

findings.title: Unclear node name: 'Node1'
findings.recommendation: Rename to a descriptive verb-noun phrase (e.g. 'Fetch Customers from API').

findings.title: Unclear node name: 'Node1'
findings.recommendation: Rename to a descriptive verb-noun phrase (e.g. 'Fetch Customers from API').

findings.title: Node 'Dangling Cleanup' is disabled
findings.recommendation: Remove if dead code; document if intentional.
